digest.component_or_area: store-level compatibility record and fail-closed capability gate
propositions: Add a committed store-level compatibility record with format_version and required_capabilities, written atomically and committed to the tickets branch, read by a fail-closed gate before any mutating or externally-publishing operation.

The gate must handle four record states: absent = legacy pass-through (not blocked), present+compatible = pass, present+incompatible = fail-closed, and corrupt/unreadable = fail-closed (never treated as absent).

Insert check_store_compat inside lock.acquire() as the single structural choke point covering all lock-based mutations, plus explicit gates in fsck_recover_cli and reconcile._apply_mutations (outbound Jira push) since they bypass the lock.
run_ensures must not swallow StoreIncompatibleError via its broad except Exception; add an explicit re-raise so init/MCP-boot/fsck-repair fail closed instead of a silent sweep no-op.

Read-only and diagnostic commands (fsck diagnostic, show, list, search) stay available (exit 0) under an incompatible/corrupt record, surfacing a machine-checkable compat_error object via non-raising describe_store_compat.
A rollback test proves a pre-v1.0 binary ignores the committed .store-compat.json (byte-unchanged, store operable), guaranteeing upgrade-in-place will not brick older clones.
